seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Claude Tool Use ai email assistant

content.intro: Build a reliable AI Email Assistant using Claude's native tool use capabilities to handle intent detection, draft generation, attachment processing, and send approvals. Guardrails are essential due to high-stakes risks like sending confidential data to wrong recipients or generating inappropriate tones. Vex ensures tool chains produce consistent, safe outputs across complex email workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant processes incoming emails through Claude's tool use: first detecting user intent (reply, forward, archive), then calling tools for draft generation, PII scanning, recipient validation, and attachment handling. Parallel tools may fetch sender history, check compliance rules, and generate content simultaneously. Final approval workflow uses Claude's agentic reasoning to confirm safety before sending.

Claude orchestrates tools like `detect_intent`, `generate_draft`, `scan_pii`, `validate_recipients`, and `handle_attachments`. Extended thinking traces ensure reasoning aligns with actions, preventing unintended sends. Vex wraps tool responses to verify outputs against email-specific policies.

The architecture supports multi-turn conversations where Claude reflects on tool results, resolves conflicts (e.g., PII vs. content needs), and requests human approval for high-risk actions.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Drafts
risks.description: Claude tools generate drafts containing unfiltered PII from email threads
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering on all tool outputs before approval workflow

risks.name: Wrong Recipient Send
risks.description: Parallel recipient validation tools return conflicting results
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies recipient domain matches company policy

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone Generation
risks.description: Draft generation tool produces sarcastic/hostile responses
risks.mitigation: Vex sentiment analysis ensures professional tone

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early PII scan failure causes draft generation to include unsafe content
risks.mitigation: Vex halts chain on first verification failure

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Verification for Email Tools
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Claude tool responses with Vex verification
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify

# After Claude tool execution
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    tools=tools,
    messages=messages
)

# Vex verify tool outputs
for tool_result in message.tool_calls:
    verified_output = verify(
        tool_result.input,
        policies=[
            "pii_filtering",
            "tone_appropriateness",
            "recipient_validation",
            "confidentiality_enforcement"
        ],
        context={
            "email_domain": "@company.com",
            "allowed_tones": ["professional", "friendly"],
            "max_pii_confidence": 0.8
        }
    )
    
    if not verified_output.valid:
        raise ValueError(f"Verification failed: {verified_output.issues}")
